headline: How journalists cover the Middle East war despite restrictions and the rise of AI-generated images
description: In this crisis that spans multiple regions, reporters are facing a tough challenge. Iran is almost entirely closed to foreign journalists, while in the Gulf countries, Israel and Lebanon, varying degrees of censorship and restrictions have been imposed on the press. At the same time, fake news about the conflict has spread across the internet.

headline: French trial examines Russian tax fraud scheme denounced by Magnitsky
Joël Soudron, one of France's most notorious drug traffickers, is arrested in Panama
France eyes ban on social media for under-15s
description: If the bill is passed, all social media platforms will refuse new users under 15, and pre-existing accounts that belong to under-15s will be suspended. Opponents argue that lawmakers must hold the platforms responsible, and not simply ban children from social media.

headline: Israel introduces death penalty for Palestinians accused of 'terrorist' murders
description: The law, passed by 62 lawmakers to 48, marks turning point in Israel's penal policy and further weakens the rule of law in the country.

headline: Spice up your life! Five Paris restaurants where you can eat spicy food
description: Mexican chipotle, Korean kimchi, Tunisian harissa: Whether mild or fiery, these condiments add zest to dishes from around the world. Here are five addresses in Paris and Montreuil to awaken your taste buds.

headline: The French cadets suspected of setting a boat ablaze in Japan
description: An investigation is underway into how four cadets from France's elite Saint-Cyr military academy came to be accused of setting a boat on fire while on leave in Okinawa in 2024. The incident has fueled local distrust of military personnel on the island.

Ireland gives Trump a lesson on Europe
description: During the traditional Saint Patrick's Day visit to the White House by the Irish prime minister, Micheál Martin defended his British counterpart and praised the virtues of Europe in the face of criticism from the US president.

headline: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

headline: After a foiled bombing outside Bank of America in Paris, five arrests and questions over Iran's role
description: Five people have been taken into police custody since two people were arrested on Saturday morning carrying an improvised explosive device near the bank's French headquarters.
